headline: Independent testing supports a bounded Project Ember bench test
summary: An independent laboratory reproduced sufficient torque in a comparable configuration and identified thermal derating after ten minutes. The evidence changes the prototype question, but does not support deployment.
checked: Project Ember torque and duty-cycle requirements
changes: Independent testing measured 105 Nm sustained torque for ten minutes at 24 V.
The test reports thermal derating beyond ten minutes.
unchanged: Project Ember requires a twelve-minute representative duty cycle.
Deployment remains outside the evidence-supported scope.

Source.title: Independent evaluation of the Nova compact actuator

Observation.statement_text: Independent testing measured 105 Nm sustained torque for ten minutes at 24 V.
Observation.test_context: Laboratory bench test at 24 V and 22 C ambient for ten minutes.

Observation.statement_text: The actuator required thermal derating after ten minutes in the independent test.
Observation.test_context: Laboratory bench test at 24 V and 22 C ambient; derating began after ten minutes.

Evidence.excerpt: At 24 V, sustained torque was 105 Nm through minute ten.
Thermal protection reduced output after ten minutes of continuous operation.

referrals.objective: Reassess whether Project Ember should authorize a bounded actuator bench test.
referrals.reason: Independent evidence now supports the torque requirement while exposing a duty-cycle limitation.
